Purchased TP-Link Archer AX12 instead of the old TP-Link 841. Expected the speed to increase, well, probably two or three times, but was immediately impressed by the fact that the internet speed increased by an order of magnitude. The smartphone instead of the usual 30-50 Mbps showed more than 300 Mbps on the speed test. Even in the far room of the apartment, through three concrete walls, it gives an average of 150 Mbps. An old Asus laptop, more than 10 years old, showed a speed of 720 Mbps via cable, although it shows only 35 Mbps over the air. Но for it, this is also an impressive result, because now it's possible to watch a movie peacefully, without constant freezes)) Among the minuses - the absence of an off button, unless you unplug the cord from the router, disconnect the power supply from the network, or turn off the button on the extension cord. It is also possible to turn off the router through the app, but haven't figured it out yet, you need to connect to the cloud there.
Everything works very cool !
